commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2ec3de43d8021c66974bd5c9f2d30056253664c7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an auth-gated live OpenCode Go provider catalog hook, refreshes static model metadata, and extends catalog tests for fetch, cache, fallback, and list-planning behavior.

PR surface: Source +242, Tests +126. Total +368 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. A live unauthenticated endpoint check returns qwen3.7-plus, while the PR source filters live IDs through a static list that does not define that model.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed to refresh the catalog and add real behavior proof; because proof is insufficient, this should stay human-gated rather than being marked for automated repair.

target.title: feat: refresh OpenCode Go model catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a provider-scoped live catalog hook for OpenCode Go so explicit provider refreshes can read `https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1/models`.
- Keep normal browse/default listing fast by preserving static/offline rows and putting the network call behind the provider catalog hook with auth gating, a 5s timeout, a 60s cache, and static fallback on failure.
- Add the current OpenCode Go models and correct transport/capability metadata for MiniMax, Qwen, MiMo, and HY3 rows.
- Cover auth gating, marker vs resolved discovery key handling, live response parsing, cache reuse, and fallback behavior in tests.

## How it works

OpenCode Go still exposes static provider-owned metadata for runtime resolution and offline catalog entries. When provider-scoped catalog discovery runs and an OpenCode/OpenCode Go key is configured, the plugin calls `/models`, dedupes model IDs, filters the provider-owned metadata to the live IDs, and returns that provider config. If auth is missing, the hook returns `null`; if the endpoint fails or returns no usable known IDs, OpenClaw keeps the static catalog so model listing does not disappear.

The request uses `discoveryApiKey` for the Authorization header but preserves the configured `apiKey` marker on the returned provider config, matching other auth-required provider catalog hooks.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts src/commands/models/list.source-plan.test.ts src/commands/models/list.provider-catalog.test.ts src/commands/models/list.list-command.forward-compat.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/opencode-go/index.ts extensions/opencode-go/provider-catalog.ts extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- Live endpoint check: `https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1/models` returned 17 model IDs, including `minimax-m3`, `qwen3.7-max`, `mimo-v2-pro`, `mimo-v2-omni`, and `hy3-preview`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenCode Go provider-scoped model catalog discovery now follows the live `/models` list without adding network fetches to normal/default browse paths.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, with current live OpenCode Go `/models` endpoint checked on June 3, 2026.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Commands listed in Verification above, plus a direct curl of `https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1/models` to confirm the current 17-model live catalog.

Evidence after fix: Targeted tests cover the live response parser, auth gate, resolved discovery secret header, cache reuse, static fallback, and model metadata for newly exposed rows. Model-list planning tests cover that broad unfiltered listing does not load runtime provider catalogs.

Observed result after fix: The live endpoint returns 17 IDs and the provider code can refresh to those known rows while preserving static fallback behavior.

What was not tested: No live authenticated OpenCode Go completion request was sent for every newly listed model.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
